jokes.text: I did not know he was a substitute teacher, and basically, nothing happened.
jokes.setup: Father brings up email from school
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately confesses to inappropriate teacher encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Alexis immediately assumes trouble is about her romantic mishaps, not lice. Strong misdirection.

jokes.text: She doesn't look like a horse, she just talks about them a lot. And smells a bit.
jokes.setup: Defending against accusation of calling classmate horse-faced
jokes.punchline: Clarifies insult while adding new insult about smell
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - defends one insult by adding another. Classic Alexis obliviousness.

jokes.text: Can everyone stop saying lice please?!
jokes.setup: Family repeatedly saying 'lice'
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted outburst becomes episode title
jokes.explanation: Title reference moment. David's squeamishness perfectly captured in economical outburst.

jokes.text: This is the last time I take a pity selfie with Kelsey!
jokes.setup: Alexis dealing with lice consequences
jokes.punchline: Reveals her 'friendship' with Kelsey was charitable photography
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - Alexis's kindness is performative and social media driven. 'Pity selfie' is a perfect original phrase.

jokes.text: Some of our guests prefer morning and evening sheets.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to change hotel sheets secretly
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous excuse about twice-daily sheet preferences
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Johnny digs deeper into unconvincing lie. Character-appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: Your hair's in a ponytail, that's fantastic... gives the neck a chance to breathe
jokes.setup: Johnny desperately trying to avoid mentioning lice
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-complimenting of practical hairstyle
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment. Johnny's desperation to find non-lice reasons for hair choices.

jokes.text: I'm not used to that level of kindness.
jokes.setup: Stevie greets him normally
jokes.punchline: David treats basic politeness as shocking kindness
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - dramatic interpretation of normal social interaction. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Mama Oprah would be very proud of us.
jokes.setup: Discussing their professional growth
jokes.punchline: David's bizarre maternal reference to Oprah
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd David moment. 'Mama Oprah' is perfectly weird and quotable.

jokes.text: A platonic sleepover?
jokes.setup: David suggesting hanging out at Stevie's
jokes.punchline: Adult describing sleepover as 'platonic'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - David's formal/awkward approach to asking for crash space.

jokes.text: That would be a shower cap.
jokes.setup: David claims he's wearing a hat
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry identification of obvious shower cap
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan. Visual comedy of David obviously wearing shower cap while claiming it's a hat.

jokes.text: Yes, that is almost entirely correct.
jokes.setup: Stevie asking if sleepover request is lice-related
jokes.punchline: David's non-denial that admits everything
jokes.explanation: Excellent David line - technically honest while completely confirming Stevie's suspicion.

jokes.text: Bob's Bench... So is the bench.
jokes.setup: Roland listing town's named landmarks
jokes.punchline: Bob confirms his bench is also a teen hookup spot
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - even the modest bench becomes part of the hookup joke pattern.

jokes.text: Meredith! She's new-ish, you might not remember her, just kind of like, mid-length, like wavy hair.
jokes.setup: Alexis inventing a client with lice
jokes.punchline: Generic, vague description of fictional person
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Alexis's transparent lie with deliberately vague details.

jokes.text: You know that it's me, right?
jokes.setup: Extended charade about fictional client 'Meredith'
jokes.punchline: Alexis finally admits the obvious truth
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Meredith charade. Alexis's eventual honesty lands well.

jokes.text: I don't know about little, because I'm benching 225 right now
jokes.setup: Alexis calls Ted a 'little vet'
jokes.punchline: Ted's insecure response about his weightlifting
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Ted. Unexpected masculine insecurity about being called 'little.'

jokes.text: Also, I'm kind of a samurai when it comes to a metal comb, so...
jokes.setup: Ted offering professional help with lice
jokes.punchline: Describing himself as a 'samurai' with a lice comb
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected metaphor - comparing lice removal to martial arts mastery is perfectly absurd and quotable

jokes.text: Um, you know that it's me, right? - Yeah, caught on pretty quick.
jokes.setup: Alexis finally admitting her elaborate lie
jokes.punchline: Ted's casual revelation he knew all along
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Alexis's elaborate deception - Ted was humoring her the whole time

jokes.text: Can you drink these?
jokes.setup: Product labeled 'body milk'
jokes.punchline: Stevie's literal interpretation of confusing name
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about confusing product naming. Very Stevie question.

jokes.text: Anyone with a fiber of common sense would know that it's not actually milk.
jokes.setup: David defending his product naming
jokes.punchline: Condescending response to obvious confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect David arrogance. Sets up Patrick's callback beautifully.

jokes.text: Oh well, anyone with a fiber of common sense would know that.
jokes.setup: David's earlier condescending comment about common sense
jokes.punchline: Patrick throws David's phrase back at him
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate callback. Patrick's dry wit perfectly deployed against David's pretension.

jokes.text: I like him. I like you.
jokes.setup: Patrick burns David with his own phrase
jokes.punchline: Stevie's rare display of approval
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie moment. Her rare enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's almost as if you want me to get the lice.
jokes.setup: Patrick gives practical advice about lice prevention
jokes.punchline: David's paranoid interpretation of helpful warning
jokes.explanation: Great David paranoia. Turns practical advice into personal attack.

jokes.text: This is really fun for me. I'm having a lot of fun.
jokes.setup: David and Patrick competing for her couch
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry enjoyment of the awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Stevie deadpan. Her rare admission of enjoyment makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Who the fuck is Lucy Albion?!
jokes.setup: Learning theatre was renamed after volunteer usher
jokes.punchline: Moira's outraged profanity about unknown person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira outrage. The F-bomb lands perfectly with her theatrical delivery style.

jokes.text: I won't be pitied, John! Or fed your pacifying pablum like some kind of soft-headed infant!
jokes.setup: Johnny's garden gesture revealed as pity
jokes.punchline: Moira's alliterative outrage with 'pacifying pablum'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary display. 'Pacifying pablum' is perfectly pretentious alliteration.

jokes.text: The notion of that garden is as abhorrent to me as our daughter's scalp!
jokes.setup: Moira rejecting the garden idea
jokes.punchline: Comparing garden to lice-infested daughter's head
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira comparison. Ties garden plot to lice plot with perfect maternal cruelty.

jokes.text: I could've sworn I heard you say change the sheets.
jokes.setup: Caught changing sheets after being told not to
jokes.punchline: Johnny's weak lie about mishearing clear instructions
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment. Johnny's continued incompetence at deception.

jokes.setup: Hotel guests asking age of lice-infected daughter
jokes.punchline: Johnny's matter-of-fact response about adult daughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Single word completely subverts expectation of child with lice.

jokes.text: Oh, and I checked your hairbrush and it is fine.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining his room service
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to touching guest's personal items
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Johnny's boundary violations. Treats invasion of privacy as service.

jokes.text: Plus I didn't want you to picture me like some well-toned bug woman.
jokes.setup: Explaining embarrassment about lice
jokes.punchline: Specific image of herself as a 'well-toned bug woman'
jokes.explanation: 'Well-toned bug woman' is a perfectly absurd Alexis phrase - she's concerned about the aesthetic even in her disgust

jokes.text: I didn't want you to picture me like some well-toned bug woman.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she lied about having lice
jokes.punchline: Bizarre self-description as 'well-toned bug woman'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Alexis line. 'Well-toned bug woman' is perfectly absurd and character-specific vanity.

jokes.text: I could be shearing you with trimmers right now.
jokes.setup: Telling Alexis it could be worse
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he might shear her like an animal
jokes.explanation: Ted's veterinary background showing through - treating Alexis like an animal patient

jokes.text: It reminds me of when I was in Bangkok. There was this amazing little Resto-Lounge that specialized in Tahitian food, and scalp massages.
jokes.setup: Ted doing lice treatment reminding Alexis of something
jokes.punchline: Bizarre restaurant concept combining food and scalp treatments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis tangent - finding sophisticated comparison for disgusting situation

jokes.text: I'm not sure what's more disgusting, what I'm doing right now, or the concept for that restaurant.
jokes.setup: Alexis's Bangkok restaurant story while Ted removes lice
jokes.punchline: Ted comparing lice removal to restaurant's hygiene nightmare
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor highlighting the absurdity of Alexis's reference point

jokes.text: Just-just the mama, we got her.
jokes.setup: Ted finding particularly large louse
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to 'mama' louse like it's a hunting trophy
jokes.explanation: Vet expertise applied to gross human situation with inappropriately casual tone

jokes.text: For some reason I pictured you living underground.
jokes.setup: David seeing Stevie's apartment
jokes.punchline: Reveals he imagined her in bunker-like conditions
jokes.explanation: Good David characterization. His assumption about Stevie's living situation is perfectly condescending.

jokes.text: I followed Lilith Fair for two summers.
jokes.setup: Defending against dissing Sarah McLachlan
jokes.punchline: David's unexpected feminist music festival history
jokes.explanation: Good character detail. David's past as Lilith Fair follower adds depth and humor.

jokes.text: Two toothbrushes? Hello!
jokes.setup: David snooping in Stevie's bathroom
jokes.punchline: Dramatic discovery of evidence of romance
jokes.explanation: Good David nosiness. His dramatic 'Hello!' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I just see two de-shelled hard-boiled eggs in a bag, should I be scared for you?
jokes.setup: David exploring Stevie's fridge
jokes.punchline: Horrified reaction to sad meal prep
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy. David's horror at Stevie's depressing food situation.

jokes.text: Oh, in the freezer, okay. There she is! Look at that, look at her, go. What a gift.
jokes.setup: David finding vodka in Stevie's freezer
jokes.punchline: His disproportionate excitement and reverence for alcohol
jokes.explanation: David's dramatic appreciation for basic apartment amenity shows his current low standards

jokes.text: Evacuation Route 14... 'Moira Rose Boulevard.'
jokes.setup: Moira listing her naming honors
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'road' was just an evacuation route she renamed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira delusion. Taking credit for emergency route as personal honor.

jokes.text: Last year my throat almost completely shut.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn's yearly allergic reaction to coconut macaroons
jokes.punchline: Escalates from rash to near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the macaroon story. Jocelyn's commitment to politeness despite danger.

jokes.text: Guest bedroom, what is he, Bill Gates?
jokes.setup: David mentioning Patrick's guest bedroom
jokes.punchline: Stevie's sarcastic comparison to billionaire
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie line. Her working-class perspective on Patrick's middle-class normalcy.

jokes.text: The Moira's Rose's Garden.
jokes.setup: Garden plaque revealed
jokes.punchline: Completely mangled grammar with multiple apostrophes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag. The grammatical disaster is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: The Moira's Rose's Garden. / So the garden is dedicated to a rose that Moira owns?
jokes.setup: Poorly punctuated garden sign with multiple possessive apostrophes
jokes.punchline: Alexis's logical interpretation of the grammatical mess
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy from grammatical error creating absurd interpretation

jokes.text: Those are the last four digits of your father's credit card.
jokes.setup: Numbers on plaque assumed to be birthday
jokes.punchline: Reveals Johnny used credit card digits instead
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the plaque disaster. Johnny's incompetence reaches new heights.

jokes.text: So when we die, are we all gonna be buried here?
jokes.setup: Family looking at the memorial-like garden
jokes.punchline: David's morbid question about family burial plot
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor ending. David's practical but morbid question caps the garden disaster.
